constraints: Allow chained direction changes pursuit escape traversal and speedrun routing
Keep exact baseline count recharge timing distance acceleration and chain cadence as playtest variables
Permit easier modes to regenerate faster without changing the core mechanic
Prevent near-continuous panic boosting while preserving frequent deliberate use
Survival primarily comes from physical displacement
Allow a very brief startup forgiveness window or small temporary hitbox reduction
Do not provide full-duration universal immunity
Define explicit interactions for walls hazards beams persistent effects and explosions
Allow easier modes to widen and extreme modes to reduce or remove the window
Communicate protection and bypass rules clearly
Allow firing in one direction while boosting sideways backward or diagonally
Map directly to keyboard gamepad and later Android movement controls
Use a deterministic neutral-input fallback with last valid movement direction as the working default
Preserve twin-stick mastery and routing
Initial direction determines most of the trajectory
Allow gentle correction or curvature during the burst
Do not permit instant reversals U-turns or free flight
Use steering to reduce controller and touchscreen imprecision
Keep steering strength and response as playtest variables
Allow shallow skims and stronger steep-angle rebounds
Keep ricochets deterministic enough to learn and reproduce
Require speed caps corner handling iteration limits anti-stuck safeguards and cramped-room testing
Do not allow uncontrolled pinball behaviour
Surface angle and incoming velocity define the main rebound
Allow movement input to bend the exit angle slightly
Preserve learnable wall geometry
Do not let input create arbitrary direction resets
Keep influence strength tunable
A valid ricochet may later restore bounded boost time or momentum
Use strict caps or diminishing returns
Do not prioritize deep implementation before the core game is usable
Reject infinite bouncing permanent propulsion encounter trivialization and unstable map skips
Retain only if playtesting proves meaningful feel value
Include simple deterministic wall reflection early
Use it to validate collision architecture room geometry and movement feel
Defer extensions chains momentum bonuses elaborate effects and exploit tuning
Allow the initial implementation to be plain
Treat D-107 as a later optional experiment
Start each spent charge timer immediately
Do not reset earlier timers when another charge is used
Keep single boosts frequent while full-bank use creates exposure
Show independent progress clearly
Use recharge duration as a primary anti-spam lever
Give every normal build the complete signature movement system
Allow at most one extra charge through rare controlled progression or specialization
Make the extra charge compete with strong alternatives
Require ordinary routes and encounters to work with baseline capacity
Record constrain or normalize capacity for verified categories
Leave exact baseline count and upgrade source to later testing

open_questions: Base locomotion acceleration and inertia between bursts
Exact baseline thruster charge count after playtesting
Exact recharge time burst distance acceleration and steering curve
Whether ricochet extensions survive post-usable-build playtesting
Exact extra-charge source and competitive normalization

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting immediate tight velocity changes, momentum-heavy inertial movement, or a responsive hybrid with quick acceleration and bounded drift. Preserve the signature chainable thruster burst, independent aiming, weapon-specific movement effects, keyboard and gamepad controls, later Android controls, broad difficulty accessibility, deterministic speedrunning, and future co-op. Include aiming stability, dodge precision, movement satisfaction, controller and touch feel, interaction with recoil and ricochets, level-design implications, accessibility, tuning complexity, and risk of the base movement making the thruster redundant or mandatory. Put the recommendation after all options. Queue D-111 for the D-120 persistence batch unless the user changes the batching rule.
